abstract: Automatic recommendation systems based on deep neural networks have become extremely popular during the last decade. Some of these systems can however be used for applications which are ranked as High Risk by the European Commission in the A.I. act, as for instance for online job candidate recommendation. When used in the European Union, commercial AI systems for this purpose will then be required to have to proper statistical properties with regard to potential discrimination they could engender. This motivated our contribution, where we present a novel optimal transport strategy to mitigate undesirable algorithmic biases in multi-class neural-network classification. Our stratey is model agnostic and can be used on any multi-class classification neural-network model. To anticipate the certification of recommendation systems using textual data, we then used it on the Bios dataset, for which the learning task consists in predicting the occupation of female and male individuals, based on their LinkedIn biography. Results show that it can reduce undesired algorithmic biases in this context to lower levels than a standard strategy.

ob.ai_reason: AI公平性+求职推荐系统，强相关管理
ob.tldr: 针对多分类神经网络在职业推荐中的性别偏见问题，提出基于最优传输理论的模型无关去偏策略，可适用于任何多分类网络结构。在LinkedIn简历预测职业的Bios数据集上验证了该方法的有效性，表明其降低职业预测中不希望的算法性别偏见的水平，优于标准策略，且为应对欧盟AI法案对高风险应用合规认证需求提供了技术路径。

title: How optimal transport can tackle gender biases in multi-class neural-network classifiers for job recommendations?
title_zh: 最优传输如何解决工作推荐中多类神经网络分类器的性别偏见
